name: Project & Work Management

users.context: Plans scope, schedule, and budget; assigns work, tracks progress against the baseline, manages risks/issues and stakeholder communication. Lives in plans, dashboards, and status meetings; juggles competing priorities.
users.expertise: Expert in PM methodology (waterfall/agile/hybrid), dependencies, and critical path; high tool fluency.

users.context: Receives and updates tasks, logs time, raises blockers, and collaborates on deliverables. Wants minimal admin overhead and a clear view of what to do next.
users.expertise: Intermediate; cares about clarity of assignment and low-friction updates, not project-management theory.

users.context: Facilitates sprints, manages the board, removes impediments, tracks velocity and burndown. Runs ceremonies and protects flow.
users.expertise: Expert in agile practice and board mechanics; needs fast board manipulation and metrics.

users.context: Prioritises the backlog, defines acceptance, approves deliverables, and tracks outcomes. Time-poor, decision-focused, often mobile.
users.expertise: Expert in the product domain; consumes roadmaps and progress summaries, drills into risks.

users.context: Allocates people across projects, monitors capacity vs. demand and utilisation, and balances the portfolio against strategy and budget.
users.expertise: Expert in capacity planning and portfolio governance.

users.context: Tracks project budgets, actuals, forecasts, and billing; enforces governance gates and reporting standards.
users.expertise: Expert in project accounting and PMO governance.

workflows.name: Project planning and baselining
steps: Build schedule with tasks, durations, and dependencies
Set budget and baseline the plan

workflows.name: Task assignment and status tracking
steps: Create task with description and acceptance
Owner updates status and logs progress/time
Close and roll up to project status

workflows.name: Status reporting and stakeholder review / gate approval
steps: Aggregate progress, budget, and RAG status
Surface risks, issues, and decisions needed
Approve phase gate or change request

risks: Mis-assigned task or unclear ownership causing work to be dropped
Hidden or broken dependency causing schedule slip and cascade delays
Inaccurate status (green when actually red) masking project trouble until too late
Scope creep accepted without a controlled change/approval
Resource over-allocation or double-booking burning out people and missing dates
Budget overrun undetected due to lagging actuals vs. forecast
Missed deadline or gate due to no deadline visibility or escalation
Wrong permission letting someone alter the baseline or others' tasks
Lost context when comments/decisions are not captured against the work item
Notification overload causing real blockers and mentions to be missed
environment_constraints: Mobile glance for approvals and status by busy stakeholders
Information overload risk from notifications and updates
Integration with code, docs, chat, and calendar tools
Pressure to minimise administrative update burden
domain_patterns: Kanban board with drag-and-drop state transitions
Gantt/timeline with dependencies and critical path
Assignment, due date, and ownership semantics
RAG/status roll-up from task to portfolio
audit_compliance: Change-control and approval audit trail for scope/baseline
Time-tracking for billing and labour/regulatory records
Data-residency/privacy for personal data in tasks (GDPR where relevant)
SOC 2 for SaaS PM tools handling customer data
Retention of project records per contractual/regulatory need
accessibility_concerns: Drag-and-drop boards must have a full keyboard-operable alternative
RAG status must not rely on red/amber/green colour alone (add labels/icons)
Dense Gantt/timeline views need screen-reader-friendly structure
Clear focus order and ARIA for interactive board cards
Sufficient contrast and target size on dense planning grids
anti_patterns: RAG and priority encoded only by colour
Drag-only boards with no keyboard path (excludes keyboard/AT users)
Vague ownership ('the team') instead of a single accountable owner
Letting status be set manually green with no link to real progress signals
Allowing baseline/scope edits without change-control or audit
Notification floods that bury genuine blockers and mentions
Burying due dates and dependencies so slips surface late
Over-granular mandatory time logging that drives avoidance and bad data
